title: Odontochilus bakhimensis
intro: Odontochilus bakhimensis, also known as Bakhim's Odontochilus, is a perennial herbaceous plant species in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Odontochilus bakhimensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Odontochilus bakhimensis is often made into herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and powders to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Odontochilus bakhimensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. More specifically, it is a member of the genus Odontochilus, which is a group of orchids native to the Himalayan region. The plant is often found growing in moist, shaded areas, such as forests and grasslands. Odontochilus bakhimensis is a relatively rare species and is not widely cultivated or studied. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and its place within the orchid family.

Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems.
